senses.gloss: a title given to people who have performed the pilgrimage to Mecca
a title given to people who have performed a pilgrimage to Jerusalem
camelthorn, manna tree, Alhagi gen. et spp.

examples.text: أَلَمْ تَرَ إِلَى الَّذِي حَاجَّ إِبْرَاهِيمَ فِي رَبِّهِ

etymology.text:
Derived from the active participle of the verb حَجَّ (ḥajja, “to go on a pilgrimage”).

Borrowed from Aramaic חָגָּה / ܚܓܐ (ḥāggā, “Alhagi”), from Akkadian 𒂊𒄖𒌋 (egû, “Alhagi”).
